privileged_info_summary: The task operates on three real, historically significant papers from computer science: (1) 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' by Claude E. Shannon (Bell System Technical Journal, 1948), the foundational work on information theory; (2) 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' by Alan Turing (Mind, 1950), introducing the Turing test; and (3) 'A Method for the Construction of Minimum-Redundancy Codes' by David A. Huffman (Proceedings of the IRE, 1952), introducing Huffman coding. All metadata claims about author names are correct: Shannon, Claude E.; Turing, Alan; and Huffman, David A. The task requires fixing Zotero database field modes from single-field (organization) format to two-field (person) format for proper citation formatting.
